narration: Your friends are quietly one of your biggest hidden expenses. The average American spends $3,200 a year on social obligations — and they never see it coming.
visual: Two person silhouettes facing each other on dark background. Dollar bill rectangles fly from left person's wallet toward right (labeled SOCIAL LIFE). Counter animates $0 to $3,200. Title: FRIENDSHIP HAS A PRICE TAG.

narration: Start with weddings. Gift, hotel, travel, and a new outfit run over $800 per wedding — and you're probably heading to three or four of them this year.
visual: Two interlocked wedding rings spring in on light background. A suitcase icon below with TRAVEL + HOTEL label. Orange badge: $800+ PER WEDDING. Subtitle: JUST ONE WEDDING, $800+ GONE.

narration: Then the group trip. Your brain literally cannot say no — a single weekend getaway runs $1,500, and FOMO is your most expensive personality trait.
visual: Airplane silhouette slides in from left on dark background. Price tag badge drops in: GROUP TRIP $1,500. Text overlay: YOUR BRAIN: I CAN'T MISS OUT.

narration: Then the small stuff. Birthday dinners, baby showers, going-away parties — about ten of these a year at $100 each quietly drains another thousand dollars.
visual: Birthday cake SVG springs in on light background. Three event cards slide in: Birthday Dinners ~$80, Baby Showers ~$120, Going-Away Parties ~$100. Total label: 10 SMALL EVENTS = $1,000/YEAR.

narration: Two weddings, one trip, ten small events — you just spent $3,200 with a smile on your face. That's more than most Americans have in savings.
visual: Dark breakdown card reveals line by line: 2 WEDDINGS $1,300 / 1 GROUP TRIP $900 / SMALL EVENTS $1,000. Total counter animates in red to $3,200.

narration: The fix is easy — set a flat $250 social budget every month, and when it's gone, it's gone. The friends who matter won't care.
visual: Piggy bank SVG springs in on light background. Orange badge: SET YOUR SOCIAL BUDGET / $250 / PER MONTH. Red text: WHEN IT'S GONE IT'S GONE. FOLLOW FOR MORE MONEY TRAPS fades in.
